layman_headline: Ethereum bouncing hard from extreme fear — a classic contrarian setup is forming.
layman_explanation: The entire crypto market is gripped by extreme fear (25/100), which historically has been one of the best times to buy. Ethereum just surged 11.4% in a week while its RSI is still near oversold at 35, meaning it has room to run before getting overheated. Bitcoin hit $65.5K on surprise inflation data, and ETH is riding that wave with strong buying conviction showing in its candle patterns. South Korea classifying crypto as national assets and Japan's regulatory overhaul add institutional tailwinds.
layman_action: Consider a cautious entry into Ethereum around $1,900, setting a hard stop-loss at $1,780. If it reaches $2,150, take profits. This is a contrarian bet during extreme fear — don't risk more than you can afford to lose.
expert_reasoning: ETH RSI at 35.01 is a high-conviction signal per our reliability data (80% win rate on RSI signals). The Bullish Marubozu candle pattern confirms strong buying conviction with zero seller pushback. While ETH is below all major MAs (bearish structure), the extreme fear environment at 25 with an improving trend from 22 yesterday suggests we're at a sentiment inflection point — Fear & Greed has a 71% win rate in our signal history. The 11.4% weekly move is aggressive but RSI hasn't caught up, suggesting this is early-stage momentum rather than exhaustion. CoinTelegraph's coverage of BTC hitting $65.5K on inflation data (80% reliable source) validates the macro catalyst. The risk is BTC dominance at 56.3% continuing to compress alts, but ETH's relative strength this week suggests capital rotation may be beginning.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.3% means capital is flowing to Bitcoin over alts — ETH could underperform if this trend continues
ETH is below all major moving averages, meaning this bounce could be a dead cat rally within a larger downtrend
11.4% weekly gain may attract profit-takers, and the nearby resistance at $1,800 (chart level) could cap upside short-term
watch_list: BTC price action around $65K-$66K — a rejection here could drag ETH back down
ETH/BTC ratio — watch for sustained improvement above 0.030 to confirm alt rotation
US Senate crypto bill progress from White House meetings — regulatory clarity could be a major catalyst or headwind
market_context: The market is in Extreme Fear (25/100) but improving from 22 yesterday, with BTC rallying to 3-week highs on surprise inflation data. High BTC dominance at 56.3% remains a headwind for alts but ETH's 11.4% weekly surge suggests early rotation may be underway.
context_banner: Extreme Fear with improving momentum and major regulatory catalysts from Asia create a high-conviction contrarian entry window for Ethereum.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 35.01 is near oversold territory despite an 11.4% weekly rally, indicating the move has room to continue — RSI signals have an 80% win rate in our track record.
Extreme Fear at 25 improving from 22 yesterday — contrarian buy signal with 71% historical win rate in our data.
BTC hitting $65.5K on surprise US inflation data is the macro catalyst lifting the entire market — CoinTelegraph signals have 80% win rate.
ETH 24h volume at $12.8B is exceptionally high relative to other alts, confirming institutional-grade participation in this bounce.
South Korea classifying crypto as national assets and Japan's regulatory overhaul provide structural bullish catalysts for institutional adoption.
